channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Partner Re | Understanding ageing in APAC: why perception, planning and protection don’t always align
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Japan insurers’ major nat cat claims hit US$464 in FY25, Kyushu floods drive 91% of losses: GIAJ
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA reviewing whether APRs support consumers’ choices
item.description: The FCA is reviewing whether Annual Percentage Rates (APRs) help consumers understand borrowing costs and is seeking views on whether it should change how these are communicated in credit advertising. To help people navigate their financial lives, we’re asking for views on whether there’s a better way.' The Discussion Paper published today, alongside the Consultation Paper on stripping back overly prescriptive requirements, focuses on whether more flexible ways of presenting loan costs could help borrowers make better informed choices. APRs indicate the yearly cost of borrowing, including interest and fees.

item.title: LCM Family Limited enters administration
item.description: On 28 April 2026, LCM Family Limited (LCM) went into administration. The joint administrators are responsible for managing the affairs of the firm during the administration process. LCM (previously known as LCM Wealth Management Limited) is authorised by the FCA and provided financial advice and related investment services.

item.title: Why Japan’s Most Durable Asset May Not Be Made In A Factory
item.description: When I was a child growing up in Japan, Dragon Ball was “contraband.” My parents were unhappy about me reading manga for hours every day. The country globally perceived as the ultimate collectivist society made its greatest contributions to the world through lone visionaries building what no committee would have approved. Japan’s extraordinary concentration of underleveraged assets, from precision manufacturing expertise, materials science technology, longevity and gastronomical research to a generational cultural content library, is the sediment left by people society once called misfits.

item.title: Seed Funding Is Bigger Than Ever — And Harder To Get
item.description: While more funding is going into startups at this stage, seed funding saw a marked shift in 2025: More than half of seed dollars last year went into deals of $10 million or above. The data points to a growing dichotomy: For your typical young startup, it’s an increasingly challenging funding landscape, despite more seed investors writing bigger checks. At the same time, deal counts for seed-stage startups have fallen since the 2021-2022 peak, as has funding going into rounds below $10 million, Crunchbase data shows.

item.title: UK Gov to legislate for risk transformation and ILS flexibility to promote innovation and growth
item.description: The Government of the United Kingdom through HM Treasury has now published its response to the July 2025 consultation on a more flexible and extended risk transformation regime, saying it will pass reforms through legislature to promote insurance-linked securities (ILS) innovation in the country and facilitate a wider range of transaction structures. Respondents encouraged continued refinement of the regime to support proportionate and efficient treatment of risk transformation, noting that flexibility in supervisory processes, wider measures on tax and government support all influence the attractiveness of the regime,” the consultation response published today explains. We reported back in July 2025 that the UK Government had launched a consultation on its Risk Transformation Regulations , citing a goal to make the regime, which is largely applicable to catastrophe bonds and insurance-linked securities (ILS), more flexible and to also allow for more ILS transaction types.

item.title: Convex reduces Hypatia 2026-1 retro cat bond size target to between $150m – $175m
item.description: Now, we understand that Convex has pared back its target size to between $150 million and $175 million, while the tranche of Hypatia Ltd. Convex Group, the expansive global specialty insurance and reinsurance underwriter, has reduced its target for the Hypatia Ltd. (Series 2026-1) catastrophe bond transaction to between $150 million and $175 million of retrocession, while the company aims to secure mid-guidance pricing for the tranche of notes on offer, this publication has learned.

item.title: Palomar raises target for Torrey Pines Re 2026-1 catastrophe bond to as much as $410m
item.description: Palomar Insurance Holdings has raised the target to as much as $410 million of capital markets backed California earthquake and Hawaii named storm reinsurance from its new Torrey Pines Re Ltd. Palomar Insurance returned to market earlier this month , initially seeking $375 million of California earthquake and Hawaii named storm reinsurance protection with what will be its seventh in the Torrey Pines Re program of catastrophe bonds. is now offering investors between $380 million and as much as $410 million of Series 2026-1 catastrophe bond notes across the four tranches.

item.title: ACORD names Tim Ellis as UK Managing Director, outlines expansion of advisory services
item.description: ACORD, the global standards-setting body for the insurance industry, has announced the appointment of Tim Ellis as Managing Director – UK. “I’ve been working with the ACORD Data Standards for decades, and I believe that now, more than ever, they are the key to building a digital future for our industry.” According to ACORD, Ellis previously served as Operations Director at Web Connectivity Limited, where his responsibilities included business transformation initiatives, stakeholder engagement, software delivery, and information security. The organisation develops data standards to support consistent and efficient information exchange across insurance markets, and says Ellis will focus on advancing the uptake and evolution of ACORD Data Standards while supporting digital transformation across the London Market and international re/insurance and specialty sectors.

item.title: The Fidelis Partnership names David Woods as Ireland CEO
item.description: The Fidelis Partnership (TFP), a specialty insurance and reinsurance group, has announced the appointment of David Woods as Ireland CEO, effective 1st July 2026. Rob Kelly, TFP’s current Ireland CEO, will transition to the role of Group Director of Finance, further strengthening the group’s finance function. In May 2026, Woods will relocate to Dublin.

item.title: Aon identifies gap between AI ambition and workforce investment in Human Capital Trends Study
item.description: Aon, a global professional services firm, has released its first Human Capital Trends Study, highlighting what it describes as a gap at the core of enterprise AI strategies. By closing the gap between ambition and readiness, leaders can act with confidence, strengthen long-term resilience and win today and in the future.” Aon states that as organisations increase AI investment, many are introducing technology faster than they are building the supporting skills, structures and human frameworks needed to make it effective. Aon indicates that while organisations widely accept that people will determine the success of AI, investment in workforce strategies is not keeping pace.

item.title: Iran War Fuels Piracy Surge Off Somalia Coast, EU Agency Says
item.description: The war in Iran is fueling a resurgence in maritime piracy off the coast of Somalia, according to a European Union naval force patrolling the region. Somalia passed legislation last year to combat piracy, but relies on foreign militaries to curb the menace as it lacks the capacity to enforce it. The attacks occurred in Eyl, Mareeyo and Garacad in Somalia’s semi-autonomous Puntland region, which was a pirate haven in the late 2000s.

item.title: Appeals Court Overturns $8M Defamation Award for Former Alabama Justice
item.description: An appeals court has reversed an $8.2 million defamation verdict awarded to Alabama politician Roy Moore, who sued a super PAC over a 2017 political ad detailing misconduct accusations against him. Circuit Court of Appeals ruled that Moore failed to prove the organization acted with malice, one of the legal standards for cases involving public figures. The three-judge panel vacated the defamation verdict and ordered the trial judge to enter a summary judgment in favor of Senate Majority PAC.

item.title: Newport Specialty Partners Acquires New York Personal Lines MGA Complex Coverage
item.description: Newport Specialty Partners, a new specialty insurance platform backed by Lovell Minnick Partners, announced that it has acquired Complex Coverage, Inc. (CCI), a managing general agency focused on homeowners and personal lines coverage in New York. The transaction is Newport’s first platform acquisition, which the company said establishes a foundation for its strategy of building a diversified MGA and specialty insurance distribution platform.

item.title: Marsh launches AI-powered Risk Companion suite to enhance risk decision-making
item.description: Marsh McLennan’s insurance broking arm, Marsh Risk, has unveiled Risk Companion, a new suite of AI-driven analytics tools designed to help clients manage increasingly complex and interconnected risks with greater speed and precision. “Risk Companion’s suite of connected solutions enables clients to turn insights into action across the risk lifecycle, from identification to measurement to treatment.” The launch marks a significant step forward in Marsh’s application of AI, allowing the firm to deliver more detailed insights at greater speed than traditional approaches. Marsh Risk is also set to introduce two additional solutions within the Risk Companion suite at RIMS RISKWORLD 2026, taking place from May 3–6.

item.title: BMS Group adopts mea Platform AI to accelerate digital broking and placement efficiency
item.description: The post BMS Group adopts mea Platform AI to accelerate digital broking and placement efficiency appeared first on Insurtech Insights . BMS Group has selected AI solutions from mea Platform to enhance placement efficiency and advance its digital broking capabilities across global (re)insurance operations. Martin Henley, Chief Executive Officer of mea Platform, said: “Placement efficiency is a defining advantage for leading brokers in today’s market.

item.title: Counterpart raises $50 million
item.description: Counterpart , a Los Angeles-based specialty MGA, has raised $50 million in a Series C round led by Valor Equity Partners, with continued backing from Vy Capital. The raise brings total funding to over $106 million. Founded in 2019, Counterpart focuses on management and professional liability.

item.title: Lemonade reports Q1 2026 results
item.description: Lemonade released its Q1 2026 results, ending the quarter with 3,142,581 customers, a 23% year-over-year increase. Lemonade reported a net loss of $35.8 million in the quarter, an improvement from the $62.4 million loss in Q1 2025. Pet insurance is Lemonade’s largest line of business, surpassing $500 million IFP early in the second quarter.

item.title: Novacore launches Zion Underwriting for M&A risk coverage
item.description: Novacore has launched Zion Underwriting , a transactional risk insurance program focused on complex M&A and structured corporate transactions. Zion will operate within Novacore’s new Alternative Risk segment and will be led by Michael Mora, who was named president of Alternative Risk. “Zion Underwriting represents a meaningful step in our continued effort to build specialized capabilities in areas of strong market demand.

item.title: Mapfre gana un 12,7% más hasta marzo y mejora su rentabilidad pese al impacto de las divisas
item.description: La mejora del resultado se apoya en el avance de la rentabilidad técnica, en el buen comportamiento del resultado financiero y en una evolución positiva de todas las regiones, en un contexto todavía condicionado por la volatilidad cambiaria. En Iberia, Mapfre incrementó su beneficio hasta los 138 millones de euros, un 13,9% más que un año antes. Mapfre obtuvo un beneficio neto de 311 millones de euros en el primer trimestre del año , lo que supone un incremento del 12,7% respecto al mismo periodo del ejercicio anterior, según ha informado hace unos minutos el grupo.

item.title: UGT se adhiere finalmente al Convenio de Seguros tras mejorar el acuerdo inicial
item.description: UGT ha decidido finalmente suscribir el Convenio Colectivo de Seguros tras la reunión plenaria celebrada esta semana . La decisión de firmar el acuerdo se ha adoptado tras un proceso de consulta y participación con las personas trabajadoras del sector y responde “a un planteamiento de responsabilidad orientado a consolidar los avances alcanzados”. El acto formal de firma del Convenio de Seguros está previsto para el próximo 5 de mayo.

item.title: Axa presenta un chatbot para agentes que agiliza los trámites y consultas en hogar
item.description: Axa España ha puesto en marcha un nuevo modelo de IA generativa que logra que los agentes de la compañía dispongan de una herramienta que revisa, de forma personalizada, las pólizas de hogar resolviendo cuestiones sobre coberturas y acelerando los trámites. “Cuando los clientes tienen una duda o un problema necesitan una respuesta de su agente precisa, clara y rápida, justo lo que buscamos con la puesta en marcha de este chatbot”, afirma Javier Caballero, director de Agentes de Axa España . Con este nuevo recurso, los agentes podrán reducir el tiempo de gestión de dudas, mejorar la satisfacción de los clientes y solventar cuestiones de manera mucho más rápida y eficaz durante la comercialización o la tramitación de un posible siniestro.

item.title: The Adjuster’s Year Ahead: What AI Will and Won’t Change About the Job
item.description: He’s looking for one paragraph, the moment an adjuster relied on output from an AI tool without documenting what it was, how it works or who validated it. What didn’t happen is the part that lived mostly in vendor slides: The vanishing adjuster. What Bob does with that paragraph, in written discovery, in a corporate representative deposition, in the first 20 minutes of mediation, is going to cost carriers, and not a little.

item.title: Bridge Specialty Group Launches Personal Lines Digital Marketplace
item.description: Personal lines wholesaler Bridge Specialty Group has launched a new personal lines digital marketplace. Bridge Specialty Group worked with Markel to make its excess and surplus homeowners product available in the marketplace, which supports API-enabled carrier integrations and structured workflows for markets without direct APIs. Bridge Specialty Group is an insurance wholesaler comprised of more than 28 specialty brands in more than 55 locations throughout the U.S., the United Kingdom and Europe.

item.title: Rational Market? How About ‘Dumb’ and ‘Bizarre’?
item.description: Industry executives who described insurance and reinsurance markets as “rational” in the not-so-distant past are now using words like “dumb” and “bizarre” to characterize soft-market competitor moves happening faster than expected, and in unusual places. Evan Greenberg and Rob Berkley used terms like “dumb” and “bizarre” to describe large-account property insurance and property-catastrophe reinsurance markets during their first-quarter earnings conference calls amid repeated questions by analysts about where top-line growth for their companies might come from this year. In particular, national carriers seem to be broadening their appetite, he said, juxtaposing this observation against remarks he made in recent prior quarters about competition coming from MGAs, MGUs—entities with delegated authority—backed by capacity from the reinsurance market and from Lloyd’s.

item.title: Pacific Prime promotes Paul Cheung to lead property, casualty, financial lines team in Singapore
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Partner Re | Understanding aging in APAC: why perception, planning and protection don’t always align
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Scope of Insurers Subject to Recovery and Resolution Planning Requirements in the Key Attributes: Overview of consultation responses
item.description: On 25 November 2025, the FSB published a consultation report on the Scope of Insurers Subject to the Recovery and Resolution Planning Requirements (RRP) in the FSB Key Attributes. The consultation, which ended on 6 February 2026, aimed at gathering feedback on criteria to identify insurers subject to RRP requirements, on specific circumstances that should necessitate RRP requirements, as well as on FSB guidance regarding the definition of critical functions for insurers. The FSB received 9 responses to the consultation, from both the public and the private sectors.

item.title: Scope of Insurers Subject to the Recovery and Resolution Planning Requirements in the FSB Key Attributes: Final Report
item.description: This report offers a structured approach for authorities to assess which insurers should be subject to recovery and resolution planning (RRP) requirements, consistent with the FSB’s Key Attributes. The guidance may also help authorities in determining which insurers to report to the FSB’s list of insurers subject to resolution planning standards consistent with the Key Attributes. Recovery and resolution planning requirements are a fundamental component of effective resolution regimes for financial institutions, including insurers.

item.title: FSB finalises guidance on insurers subject to recovery and resolution planning
item.description: Press enquiries: +41 61 280 8477 [email protected] Ref: 6/2026 The Financial Stability Board (FSB) today published its finalised guidance on the scope of insurers subject to recovery and resolution planning (RRP) requirements . The guidance may also help authorities in determining which insurers to report to the FSB’s list of insurers subject to resolution planning standards. The guidance offers a structured approach for authorities to assess which insurers should be subject to those requirements, consistent with the FSB Key Attributes of Effective Resolution Regimes for Financial Institutions (Key Attributes).

item.title: RenRe third-party capital ILS fee income soars to $94m in Q1 2026, investors take profits
item.description: Total net income attributable to redeemable noncontrolling interests for Q1 2026, which is a measure of sorts for returns and profits earned and attributed to third-party capital investors in the period, reached $222.5 million for the period. Capital returns outweighed this considerably though, as RenRe reports delivering a significant $930.3 million back to investors in the third-party and ILS capital strategies. It’s a sign of RenRe continuing to right-size the structures and ensuring its third-party investors benefit from the historically strong performance of certain of its third-party reinsurance capital strategies.

item.title: Danish Supreme Court ruling leads to Tryg recognising one-off impact in workers’ comp case
item.description: Tryg, a Nordic insurance company with operations in Denmark, Sweden and Norway, has commented on a ruling by the Danish Supreme Court concerning workers’ compensation in Denmark, which carries implications for the wider insurance sector as well as for the Danish State and municipalities. Tryg, together with the Danish Insurance Association, has stated its view that responsibility for the resulting industry costs should rest with the Danish State, including indemnification of insurers. Tryg notes that this represents a change from more than four decades of established administrative practice in Denmark, previously applied by public authorities in the handling of workers’ compensation claims.

item.title: RenaissanceRe returns to underwriting profit in Q1’26 amid ‘deliberate portfolio construction’
item.description: (RenRe) produced underwriting profit of $588.8 million in the first quarter of 2026, a significant improvement on the prior year’s $770.6 million loss, as gross premiums written (GPW) fell by approximately 17% year-on-year to $3.5 billion. The Property unit’s underwriting result improved to a profit of $593.9 million in Q1’26, compared with a loss of $607.2 million a year earlier. Bermuda-based reinsurer RenaissanceRe Holdings Ltd.

item.title: Marsh Risk launches AI-powered suite of analytics for faster, smarter risk management
item.description: Insurance broker Marsh Risk, a business of Marsh, has announced the launch of Risk Companion, a new suite of AI-driven analytics tools designed to help clients more effectively and efficiently manage their unique risks. This launch represents a significant advance in Marsh’s use of AI to give clients a more detailed view into their risk exposures and mitigation options, allowing the firm to deliver insights at a faster pace and in greater detail than previously available. Michelle Sartain, President US and Canada, Marsh Risk, said: “As risks have become more complex, interconnected, and volatile, clients around the world need quicker access to the data, analytics, and insights that enable them to cut through the noise and act with greater confidence at key moments.

item.title: Fast-Moving storms Batter the Midwest With Hail, Heavy Rain
item.description: Fast-moving storms pummeled parts of the Midwest with hail, strong wind and heavy rain Monday, flooding streets, stranding commuters and downing many trees, including one in western Michigan that landed on a man and killed him. More than 56 million people in the Midwest and parts of the South were at risk of severe storms, with tornado warnings posted in southeastern Missouri, southern Illinois and northern Arkansas, the National Weather Service said. He said most “were in our typical ‘high-water’ areas following heavy rains.” Nolan said no boats were required and no injuries were reported.

item.title: New MGA Ascendri Aims For High-Value Homes in Catastrophe-Exposed Areas
item.description: Ascendri Insurance Services, a new managing general agency, has launched to address high-value risks in catastrophe-exposed markets. “Our goal is to deliver strong, high‑quality protection at a competitive price—without forcing unique homes into standard boxes.” Topics Catastrophe Insurance Wholesale Homeowners Starting in California, Ascendri will provide excess & surplus homeowners coverage for homes with insured values up to $10 million, targeting a segment where it says coverage has become increasingly limited.

item.title: Data Center Boom Strains Texas Homebuilders’ Need for Electricians
item.description: Early industry projections show data centers projects will need thousands of licensed electricians, pulling from a limited labor pool. Between 45% and 70% of the entire budget for data center construction goes to electrical subcontractors, according to the International Brotherhood of Electrical Workers, a labor union that represents electricians. The data center in the Abilene area, for example, offers electricians double the wages his subcontractors can as officials aim to finish its construction by the end of the year, Lantrip said.

item.title: Duck Creek Launches Agentic AI Platform for Carriers
item.description: Duck Creek Technologies (Boston) has launched an insurance-native agentic AI platform and introduced new applications aimed at improving underwriting and claims operations for property/casualty carriers. “Our Agentic Platform combines our leadership in core systems, proprietary insurance domain ontology and expertise, and Agentic AI with neuro-symbolic reasoning to create agents that operate with full context, governance, traceability and human in the loop—so carriers can scale AI with confidence and trust.” Hardeep Gulati, CEO, Duck Creek Technologies. “Agentic AI will redefine how insurance operates—enabling carriers to move from manual, fragmented processes to orchestrated end-to-end decisioning and support for all personas to drive better outcomes and continuously improve,” comments Hardeep Gulati, Chief Executive Officer, Duck Creek.

item.title: Gramercy Risk Management Selects Origami Risk Core Platform
item.description: Gramercy Risk Management (Uniondale, NY.) has selected Origami Risk’s property/casualty platform from Origami Risk (Chicago) to support expansion and streamline core operations. “We’re honored to support Gramercy Risk Management as they take this important step in updating their core systems,” comments Mike Kaplan, Chief Client Officer, Origami Risk. “Origami Risk’s integrated, cloud-native platform gives us the flexibility to streamline operations across policy, billing, claims and risk management while supporting the launch of new programs and our evolving business needs.” Origami Risk describes its platform as providing a single environment for policy administration, rating, billing, claims, loss control, reporting, analytics, and digital engagement.

item.title: Purdue Pharma to Be Sentenced, Paving Way for Opioid Settlement
item.description: Related: Purdue Pharma Gets Court Nod for Bankruptcy Exit, Sackler Deal Purdue’s lawyer, Eli Vonnegut, said in court on Tuesday that the settlement fulfills the promises Purdue made in the bankruptcy litigation, even if victims of the opioid crisis are owed far more than the company can pay. Purdue Pharma appeared in New Jersey federal court on Tuesday for sentencing on charges of deceiving government regulators and paying kickbacks to doctors to boost opioid sales. Related: Purdue’s $5.5B Sentencing for Opioid Charges Delayed After Victims Show at Court Alexis Pleus, whose son Jeff died after he took opioids for a football injury and became addicted, told Arleo that justice would not be served by the settlement.

item.title: NYC Begins 10 Days of Flying Taxis With Manhattan-JFK Flights
item.description: The Santa Cruz, California-based company will be seeking to introduce New Yorkers to electric flights over 10 days of multiple trips. is planning to fly electric air taxis between John F. Kennedy International Airport and Manhattan this week to show off its quieter, zero-emission aircraft in a city weary of noise pollution.

item.title: Unexpected $115B+ Insured Loss Needed to Harden Property Market: Gallagher Re
item.description: “We now estimate that it would require a single event (or a series of large events) resulting in an insured loss of at least $115 billion to $125 billion – above the expected average annual catastrophe losses – to meaningfully impact the trajectory of pricing in the property sector of the industry,” Gallagher Re said. Q1 insured losses from natural disasters hit approximately $20 billion, agreed Aon and Gallagher Re in their Q1 nat cat market overviews. “The manageable start to 2026 continues a trend of lower loss quarters for insurers,” said Gallagher Re in its report titled “ Q1 2026 Gallagher Re Natural Catastrophe and Climate Report .” Gallagher Re said global insured losses of at least $20 billion were 26% below the decadal average of $26 billion.

item.title: ‘Catastrophic failure’ risk looms amid warning Shanghai floods could expand 80% by 2100
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Dallas Hail, Tornado Threats Are Grounding Hundreds of Flights
item.description: A powerful band of thunderstorms threatening softball-sized hail, tornadoes and dangerous winds are menacing Dallas and the US South, grounding hundreds of flights and knocking out power to thousands. local time, 278 flights were canceled at Dallas Fort Worth International Airport with dozens of others grounded in Chicago and Atlanta as the storms send ripples through US air traffic, according to FlightAware, an airline tracking service. Tornado watches stretch from northeast Texas to western Tennessee, and there are at least four reports of possible twisters moving across the region, according to the National Weather Service.

item.title: North Texas Tornadoes Leave 2 Dead and Destroy Multiple Homes
item.description: A tornado-producing thunderstorm left at least two people dead in northern Texas and displaced at least 20 families, with many homes sustaining major damage, authorities said Sunday. At least one person was killed and numerous homes were damaged Saturday night in the town of Runaway Bay, said Wise County Judge J.D. Emergency responders worked to clear debris to reach damaged homes and provide medical care where needed, Clark said.

item.title: Reassured hits 2M policy milestone
item.description: Reassured has reached two million life and critical illness policies sold since its 2009 launch, marking a milestone for one of the UK’s largest protection distributors. “Reassured is proud to reach this significant milestone of providing two million policies which give vital financial protection for families across the UK. Reassured attributes much of its growth to a digital strategy centered on AI and data.

item.title: Miller reports fifth year of growth
item.description: Miller reported another year of growth, with revenue rising 15% to £314 million for 2025, marking its fifth consecutive year of expansion. Growth was driven by a mix of acquisitions and geographic expansion. Miller acquired reinsurance broker AHJ, strengthening its treaty capabilities and adding exposure to Nordic, Caribbean, and North American markets.

item.title: Fintechs no vigiladas temen quedar fuera del open finance en Colombia
item.description: Las entidades no vigiladas por la Superintendencia Financiera de Colombia, entre ellas las fintechs de crédito digital, no estarán obligadas a implementar el open finance. Aunque buena parte de la industria es vigilada por la Superfinanciera, como por ejemplo los neobancos o las Sedpes (sociedades especializadas en depósitos y pagos electrónicos), Gabriel Santos, presidente ejecutivo de Colombia Fintech dijo a iupana que la participación de lo que la norma llama “terceros receptores de datos no vigilados” se realizará a través de un esquema voluntario. Así quedó definido en el Decreto 0368 que pone en marcha el sistema de finanzas abiertas en el país andino.

item.title: Neuro-Symbolic AI Addresses Insurance’s Explainability Challenge
item.description: Neuro-symbolic AI still has to solve the hardest challenge: building a structured knowledge layer from the unstructured documents real organizations actually have at enterprise scale. Gartner’s 2025 AI Hype Cycle names neuro-symbolic AI as the architecture designed for exactly this problem, automating decision-making with less risk of unintended consequences. Instead, neuro-symbolic architecture separates responsibilities.
